locked_from: .spec/scenarios (backfilled from SPRINT.md test steps of sprint-01a + sprint-01b)
replay_note: Every run_cmd drives the REAL `but` binary via assert_cmd/snapbox (real git + real but-db, no mocks). The gate adds cold-boot + altitude lints on top. Verdict computed by tools/gate-evidence/ from exit codes + fresh artifacts — NOT narrated.

flows.name: Functional permission model — authorize() resolves authority set from committed config
flows.expected: A principal with contents:write is permitted; one with only contents:read is denied with the exact Authority named. Authority comes from committed permissions.toml only.

flows.name: Absent principal denied (fail-closed default-deny)
flows.surface_lint: coldboot_check.py — config must be committed (target-ref), not seeded at test time
flows.expected: A principal whose BUT_AGENT_HANDLE is absent from committed permissions.toml → denied perm.denied.

flows.name: Agent-readable structured denial on stderr + exit 1
flows.expected: A denied governed action emits {code, message, remediation_hint} JSON on stderr + exit 1; an authorized action exits 0 and proceeds.

flows.name: Unset BUT_AGENT_HANDLE rejected — no anonymous action
flows.expected: Run a governed action with BUT_AGENT_HANDLE unset → rejected exit 1, no anonymous action runs.

flows.name: Malformed gates.toml → config.invalid (fail-closed)
flows.expected: Commit a malformed gates.toml to the target ref, run a governed action → denied config.invalid.

flows.name: Partial config (exactly one file) → config.invalid
flows.run_cmd: cargo test -p but --features but-2 'commit_gate_absent_config_is_ungoverned' -- --nocapture && cargo test -p but --features but-2 'commit_gate_malformed_partial_and_dryrun' -- --nocapture
flows.expected: Exactly one of {permissions.toml, gates.toml} → config.invalid (fail-closed). Both absent → ungoverned (allow). Opt-in-by-presence three-state model.

flows.name: Commit gate — contents:write commit on feature branch lands
flows.expected: As dev (contents:write), run but commit on a feature branch → exit 0, ref advances. Gate authorizes contents:write and target is not protected.

flows.name: Protected-branch commit denied + working-tree edit inert
flows.expected: Direct commit to protected main → branch.protected. Working-tree gates.toml edit to unprotect → still branch.protected (target-ref-only read).

flows.name: Merge gate — merge after distinct approval at head proceeds
flows.expected: As maintainer, run but merge after a distinct reviewer approval at head → exit 0, merge proceeds.

flows.name: Merge denied variants — no approval, stale, auto-merge, DryRun
flows.expected: Zero approvals → gate.review_required. Stale approval → gate.review_required + approval_stale_at_head. Auto-merge as non-merge principal → perm.denied. DryRun merge → still denied, nothing persisted.

flows.name: T-LOOP-006 reference flow — 3-principal implement→review→merge canary [JOURNEY]
flows.surface_lint: e2e_surface_check.py (enters at the real `but` CLI, not beneath) + coldboot_check.py (fresh repo, config committed as a human would — NOT seeded/injected)
flows.expected: reference_flow_full_loop + auto_merge_denied + remediation_traversable + dryrun_no_bypass + unset_handle_failclosed → 5/5 green. The implementer's merge AND auto-merge denied; maintainer's merge succeeds only after a distinct reviewer approval at head; a denied implementer following remediation_hint lands through a reviewed merge. Role separation emerges from the functional permission set.

flows.name: Remediation-hint traversal — denied implementer follows the hint and lands [JOURNEY]
flows.expected: A denied implementer follows its remediation_hint (feature branch → review → merge) and lands successfully. The irrigation thesis: the governed path is traversable.

flows.name: No role-name in any enforcement path — functional authority only
flows.expected: The enforcement path contains no role-name, no human-vs-AI predicate, no Permission overload. The build-gate honesty grep is green.

flows.name: Honesty invariant grep-gates re-asserted after AUTHZ hardening
flows.expected: After all AUTHZ hardening, re-assert: no role name, no human-vs-AI predicate, no Permission overload. Invariant grep-gates green.
